text:
Once upon a time there was a brave squirrel. He lived in an old oak tree with big holes for ears. Every day he went on adventures collecting nuts.

One day, as usual, he scampered down to the town centre. He spotted Mrs. Owl watching birds. "Aren't you owl envy-worthy?" asked Spotter.

"Hohoho!" she hooted. "Nothing delights me as much as a decent acorn!"

Just then, something shiny caught Spotter's eye. It wasn •was• a golden nut—a prize he'd never dared steal! Spotter grabbed it.

A crowd had gathered. A fox leanedowards Spotter.

"You didn't just lift that!" accused the fox.

"No!" protested Spotter.

With a crafty wink, Spotter tossed the nut back into heaven.

The owls cheered!

Their secret was safe.

That night though...something wasn't right. Spotter awoke feeling sick.

He stumbled towards his big oak. Something heavy
